message:
fix(core): correct markdown heading stripping to preserve inline hash tokens and fix hyphenated keyword classification

In canonicalize.rs: the markdown heading stripper was unconditionally
consuming '#' characters anywhere in the text, not just at line starts.
This caused "C#" and "#hashtag" tokens to lose their '#' prefix during
canonicalization.  Add `at_line_start` tracking so heading markers are
only stripped when they appear at the beginning of a line, preserving
inline hash tokens in all other positions.  Includes a new test
exercising both C# and #hashtag preservation alongside heading removal.

In query_class.rs: hyphenated terms like "error-handling" and
"load-balancer" were misclassified as Identifier (the identifier
heuristic matched "ALPHA-ALPHA" patterns).  Tighten the identifier
suffix constraint to require all-digit characters after the hyphen,
so only patterns like "JIRA-456" or "bd-123" are treated as
identifiers.  Adds a test confirming "error-handling", "load-balancer",
and "bd-ab" all classify as ShortKeyword.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
